steps.title: Identify roots and factor the quadratic
steps.content: The graph passes through $(10, 0)$ and $(-5, 0)$, which are the roots of the quadratic. A quadratic with roots $r_1$ and $r_2$ can be factored as $f(x) = a(x - r_1)(x - r_2)$. Substituting the given roots: $f(x) = a(x - 10)(x + 5)$. This form uses the fact that roots correspond to factors $x - r$.

steps.title: Expand to standard form and isolate coefficients
steps.content:
Expanding the factored form: $$f(x) = a(x - 10)(x + 5) = a(x^2 - 5x - 50)$$
Distribute $a$: $$f(x) = ax^2 - 5a x - 50a$$
Comparing to $f(x) = ax^2 + bx + c$, we find $b = -5a$. Thus, $a + b = a + (-5a) = -4a$.

steps.title: Test integer values of $a$ against choices
steps.content:
Since $a$ is an integer $>1$ and $a + b = -4a$, test possible values:
  • $a = 2 \Rightarrow -4(2) = -8$ (choice A)
  • $a = 3 \Rightarrow -12$
  • $a = 4 \Rightarrow -16$
Only choice A is listed among the options.
